channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives in programs receiving federal money has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, TERRY CHEA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sony PlayStation Network outage enrages gamers around the world
item.description: ROME (AP) — A major outage of Sony’s PlayStation Network (PSN) on Saturday has left tens of thousands of gamers unable to access online services, stores and multimedia apps.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hamas releases 3 more Israeli hostages for 183 Palestinian prisoners under Gaza ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas-led militants released three gaunt, frail-looking Israeli hostages on Saturday, and Israel freed 183 Palestinian prisoners as part of a fragile agreement that has paused the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge blocks Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing sensitive US Treasury Department material
item.description: A federal judge early Saturday blocked Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans. U.S. District Judge Paul A.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A hidden gem of whitewater rafting faces an uncertain future after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ERWIN, Tenn. (AP) — After 24 years of guiding whitewater trips on the Nolichucky River Gorge for other companies, Patrick Mannion finally received a permit last year to operate his own outfitter business.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board were dead, authorities said. The crash was one of the deadliest in the state in the last 25 years.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Trump ending Kennedy Center board members' terms, naming himself chair in place of Rubenstein
item.description: President Trump on Friday said he would end the terms of multiple members of the Kennedy Center board and make himself chair of a new board, putting himself at the helm of the Washington, D.C., cultural institution.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks USAID purge, evacuations from host countries
item.description: A federal judge late Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on leave at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Alaska Legislature asks Trump to retain Denali's name instead of change it to Mount McKinley
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The Alaska Legislature passed a resolution Friday urging President Donald Trump to reverse course and retain the name of North America’s tallest peak as Denali rather than change it to Mount McKinley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A mother and daughter were killed in tornadoes that struck eastern Tennessee
item.description: WARTBURG, Tenn. (AP) — Two tornadoes moved through eastern Tennessee, leaving a mother and daughter dead and injuring three other people, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge in Boston to consider latest bid to block Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston said on Friday he would take under advisement a request from 18 state attorneys general to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arizona grandmother indicted in death of epileptic teen kept in makeshift cage
item.description: The grandmother of a teenage girl with epilepsy who died after police said she had been locked in a makeshift cage for extended periods of time has been indicted by a grand jury in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger’s mental health records given to prosecutors as he fights death penalty
item.description: Prosecutors have been given Idaho student murders suspect Bryan Kohberger's medical and mental health records, newly unsealed court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: Inside elite SWAT team's final sprint to secure New Orleans
item.description: Ahead of Super Bowl LIX and after the Bourbon Street terror attack, officials with Homeland Security Investigations are preparing to combat any potential threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: Authorities say a small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome has been located on sea ice and all 10 on board are dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Mexico courtroom devolves into brawl as 3 people rush defendant in homicide case
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A hearing in a New Mexico courtroom devolved into chaos when three people rushed the defendant in a homicide case, setting off a brawl with flying fists and kicks. Courtroom video cameras recorded the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bond set at $20M for man accused in shooting at Ohio warehouse that killed 2 and wounded 4
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A warehouse worker said in a frantic 911 call that he was shot in the neck and then took the gun from a co-worker after he had shot several others. Two people died and four were injured in the Ohio shooting that sent panicked employees fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Connecticut's Bradley International Airport experiencing air traffic controller shortage
item.description: Numbers obtained by NewsNation affiliate WTNH show that one senator is quite concerned about airline safety.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Derderian, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 12 states to sue over DOGE access to payment systems containing personal data
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law," a statement from the Democratic attorneys general reads.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah Legislature bans collective bargaining for teachers unions and other public sector jobs
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Labor unions that serve teachers, firefighters, police and other public employees in Utah will no longer be able to negotiate on behalf of their workers under a bill that received final legislative approval Thursday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tampa police to keep close eye on Ybor's Illuminated Knight parade
item.description: Tens of thousands of people will pack the streets in Ybor City for the Illuminated Knight parade, but keeping everyone safe in a crowd that size is no small task.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Public objections stall State Library defunding bill
item.description: “I did receive a lot of letters and the outpouring from the hearts of the little children were really, really special to me,” Rep. Lana Greenfield said.
item.dc:creator: Gracie Terrall, NewsNation

item.title: Second type of bird flu found in US dairy cows
item.description: Experts said it raises new questions about wider spread and the difficulty of controlling infections in animals and the people who work closely with them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOGE officials hone in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2nd victim dies after shooting at Ohio cosmetics facility; suspect appears in court
item.description: A second person has died after a gunman opened fire in a cosmetics facility in New Albany, Ohio, late on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, Fox 5

item.title: A dozen West Point cadet clubs ordered to disband, all others paused
item.description: The U.S. Military Academy at West Point, New York, has ordered a dozen cadet extracurricular and social clubs to immediately and permanently "cease all activities," with all affected groups centered on gender, race and ethnicity, the school acknowledged Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Trump is targeting antisemitism in schools. Experts fear other civil rights will be ignored
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal office that enforces civil rights at schools across the U.S. has been ordered to prioritize complaints of antisemitism above all else as it molds to President Donald Trump’s agenda, raising fears that other rights violations will go unpunished.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday intended to ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5
